published_prior_art.cite: BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2011ConvexMoment + ManskiTamer2002IntervalData: support intervals over convex/interval feasible sets are generic partial-identification machinery
Song2024GeneralBunching and Dong2015RoundingRD are adjacent but do not give a deterministic heap-grid/window phase frontier

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_multivariate_interval_regression_v1: endpoint-pruning/dominance warning is the closest in-catalogue analogue

published_prior_art.cite: ManskiTamer2002IntervalData: unrestricted within-interval values already imply endpoint replacement is not generally sharp

structure_flags.label: BoschJongenLejour2020Window
structure_flags.one_line: The cited 2020 bunching-window paper appears to be Bosch-Dekker-Strohmaier, 'A data-driven procedure to determine the bunching window', Int Tax Public Finance 27:951-979, not Bosch-Jongen-Lejour with the bibliography title/pages shown.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: As stated, the support-interval theorem and nullspace certificate are generic convex/linear partial-identification facts already covered by the interval/random-set literature, not a new theorem.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The Gap clause cites BoschJongenLejour2020Window for bunching-window choice, but the verified paper matching that claim has different authors/title/pages.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship phase-threshold claim relies on BarrecaLindoWaddell2016HeapingRD and BoschJongenLejour2020Window only at bibkey level in the Results-at-a-glance gap; add section/page/equation anchors.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: Endpoint dominance is advertised as the sharp condition, but §9 does not compute a three-subcell endpoint-pruning witness or verify the endpoint frontier on a worked instance.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The certificate clause needs p0 feasible for the observed histogram, i.e. Ap0=q(P); otherwise it proves variation for some rounded law, not for the displayed P.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The phase rank rho is defined without requiring b^T h != 0 or b outside the relevant row span, so rho>0 alone need not imply aliasing of the target functional.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: Endpoint-cell dominance is stated informally as a directional mass-moving property for every stencil; it needs a precise inequality over cells/stencils before the iff-style endpoint claim is well posed.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Patch Theorem 1 by adding Ap0=q(P), then demote it in prose to a setup lemma rather than a novelty-bearing theorem.
Redefine the phase object as a target-sensitive rank, e.g. requiring existence of h in the heap-null tangent cone with b^T h != 0, and update the point-ID branch accordingly.
Replace the BoschJongenLejour citation with the verified Bosch-Dekker-Strohmaier window paper, or drop it if the intended comparator is different.
Add a §9 endpoint-pruning exhibit with a straddling heap cell of three latent subcells, explicit b, p0, endpoint replacement rule, and computed frontier mismatch.

recommended_next_step: Revise locally: fix the feasibility condition, make the phase rank target-sensitive, correct the Bosch citation, and add a concrete endpoint-pruning exhibit.
